_meta.purpose: Care directives keyed by visible injury, hidden risk code (Q5-Q12), and triage tag. Field AI uses entries matching the patient to seed tactical_advice. NEVER queried as RAG — pure dict lookup.
_meta.content_provenance: AI-generated for hackathon demo. All entries cross-referenced against the cited ref_category in training-time knowledge. NOT clinically validated for deployment. Real deployment requires medical-director review of every entry.
ref_categories_used: TCCC Guidelines (Tactical Combat Casualty Care, public)
PHTLS 10th ed (Prehospital Trauma Life Support, NAEMT, paywall)
ATLS 11th ed (Advanced Trauma Life Support, ACS, paywall)
ABLS Provider Manual (Advanced Burn Life Support, ABA)
PALS Provider Manual (Pediatric Advanced Life Support, AHA)
ALSO (Advanced Life Support in Obstetrics, AAFP)

key_format.Q5_crush_syndrome: hidden risk code with descriptive suffix
key_format.RED_general: triage tag general directive when no specific match

Q5_crush_syndrome.directive: Establish two large-bore IVs (14-16G) BEFORE extrication if access allows. Run 1L normal saline bolus pre-release. Place cardiac monitor — watch for peaked T waves, widened QRS, arrhythmia immediately post-release. Mark time of extrication.
do_not: Do not give potassium-containing fluids (avoid Ringer's Lactate if hyperkalemia suspected)
Do not give succinylcholine if RSI needed (use rocuronium)
Do not loosen entrapment bindings until IV access + monitor in place

Q6_occult_internal_bleed.directive: Treat as hypovolemic shock until proven otherwise. Permissive hypotension (target SBP 80-90 if no head injury). TXA 1g IV if within 3h of injury. Cardiac monitor. Prepare for rapid transport.
do_not: Do not over-resuscitate with crystalloid (worsens dilutional coagulopathy)
Do not delay transport for further assessment if mechanism suggests intra-abdominal or pelvic bleed

Q7_pregnancy_complication.directive: Position patient in left lateral decubitus (relieves IVC compression). Establish IV access. If active vaginal bleeding, count and save pads. If seizure or severe hypertension, treat as eclampsia — protect airway, do not delay transport for full workup. Document fetal movement if reported.
do_not: Do not let patient lie flat supine (IVC compression reduces cardiac output)
Do not deliver in field unless crowning visible — transport to OB-capable facility

Q8_altered_mental_status.directive: Protect airway (recovery position if no spinal concern, otherwise log-roll with C-spine control). Check glucose if test available. Pupils + GCS every 5 min. Document last known normal time. High-flow O2 if SpO2 < 94%.
do_not: Do not assume intoxication — exclude hypoglycemia, head injury, stroke first
Do not give naloxone empirically without clear opioid mechanism in trauma context

Q9_airway_burn.directive: High-flow O2 via non-rebreather mask. Sit patient upright if conscious. Watch for stridor, hoarseness, drooling — these are intubation triggers but require trained personnel. Expedite transport to burn center.
do_not: Do not intubate prophylactically without trained personnel and equipment for difficult airway
Do not give large IV fluid boluses (worsens airway edema) — follow Parkland once weight + TBSA known
Do not apply ice to facial burns

Q10_neurogenic_shock.directive: Maintain spinal precautions throughout (cervical collar + log-roll). Hypotension with bradycardia + warm-pink skin distinguishes neurogenic from hemorrhagic shock. Cautious fluid bolus 500ml NS, reassess. Document motor + sensory level.
do_not: Do not aggressively fluid-resuscitate (risk of pulmonary edema in neurogenic shock)
Do not remove cervical collar to assess
Do not log-roll without minimum 4 rescuers if practical
Q10_neurogenic_shock.ref_category: ATLS 11th Spinal Trauma Ch + AANS Guidelines

Q11_geriatric_anticoag_head.directive: Treat as high-risk intracranial bleed even if GCS 15 and asymptomatic at scene. Document medication list verbatim (warfarin, DOACs, antiplatelets). Expedite transport to CT-capable facility. Frequent re-assessment for late deterioration.
do_not: Do not clear from scene based on normal mental status alone
Do not delay transport waiting for symptom development
Q11_geriatric_anticoag_head.ref_category: Geriatric Trauma Guidelines + ATLS 11th Special Populations

Q12_blast_lung.directive: High-flow O2. Look for cyanosis, hemoptysis, subcutaneous emphysema, chest wall asymmetry. If suspected pneumothorax with hemodynamic instability — needle decompression by trained personnel only. Permissive hypotension acceptable, avoid fluid overload.
do_not: Do not give large fluid boluses (worsens pulmonary contusion)
Do not give positive-pressure ventilation aggressively if tension pneumothorax suspected
Q12_blast_lung.ref_category: CDC Blast Injury Primer + ATLS 11th Thoracic Trauma

major_hemorrhage.directive: Apply direct pressure first. If extremity bleeding uncontrolled in 30 sec, place CAT tourniquet 5-7 cm proximal to wound (not over a joint). Tighten until bleeding stops. Mark 'TQ' and exact time on the patient. TXA 1g IV if within 3h.
do_not: Do not loosen tourniquet to check bleeding once applied
Do not place tourniquet over a joint
Do not apply tourniquet for minor bleeding controllable with pressure

burn_major.directive: Stop the burning process (remove from source, smother flames, irrigate chemical with water 15+ min if no other contraindication). Calculate %TBSA by Rule of Nines. Parkland formula: 4ml * kg * %TBSA over 24h, half in first 8h from time of burn. Cover with dry sterile burn sheets. Keep patient warm.
do_not: Do not apply ice or cold water to large burns (causes hypothermia + worsens injury)
Do not break blisters
Do not apply topical creams or ointments in field
Do not delay transport for fluid resuscitation calculation

burn_minor.directive: Cool with running water for 15-20 minutes if accessible. Cover with clean dry dressing. Oral analgesia if able to swallow. Refer for follow-up if burn crosses joint, involves face/hands/perineum, or >5% TBSA.
do_not: Do not apply ice directly to skin
Do not use butter, toothpaste, or home remedies
Do not pop blisters

open_fracture.directive: Cover wound with sterile saline-moistened gauze. Splint in position found if no distal pulse compromise. Document distal pulse, motor, sensation before AND after splinting. Antibiotics within 1 hour if able (cefazolin 2g IV or equivalent).
do_not: Do not push exposed bone back under skin
Do not delay transport for definitive splinting
Do not apply tourniquet for fracture-only bleeding (use direct pressure)

closed_fracture.directive: Splint above and below the fracture in position found. Document distal neurovascular status before and after splinting. Ice + elevation if not contraindicated. Analgesia per protocol.
do_not: Do not attempt reduction unless no pulse distal AND trained
Do not over-tighten splint (compartment syndrome risk)

amputation.directive: Control bleeding with direct pressure first; tourniquet if uncontrolled. Wrap amputated part in saline-moist gauze, place in sealed bag, place bag on ice (NOT direct ice contact with tissue). Bring with patient. Time-critical for replantation.
do_not: Do not place amputated part directly on ice or in water
Do not discard the amputated part — bring it
Do not delay transport waiting for full search of small parts

chest_trauma.directive: Expose chest. Look for paradoxical movement (flail), open wound (sucking chest wound — apply three-sided occlusive dressing), asymmetric breath sounds, tracheal deviation, subcutaneous emphysema. Position semi-upright if no spinal concern. High-flow O2.
do_not: Do not seal open chest wound on all four sides (causes tension pneumothorax)
Do not give positive-pressure ventilation aggressively if tension pneumothorax not yet decompressed

head_trauma.directive: Maintain C-spine precautions. Document initial GCS. Reassess every 5 min — any drop is a high-priority finding. Position head-up 30 degrees if no spinal concern. Avoid hypoxia and hypotension (both worsen secondary brain injury). High-flow O2 if SpO2 < 94%.
do_not: Do not hyperventilate (causes cerebral vasoconstriction, worsens injury)
Do not assume intoxication — assume head injury until proven otherwise

spinal_suspected.directive: Manual in-line stabilization, then apply rigid cervical collar. Log-roll with minimum 4 rescuers if possible. Long backboard for extrication, may remove for transport per local protocol. Document motor + sensory at multiple dermatomes.
do_not: Do not remove helmet (sports/motorcycle) unless airway compromised and no spare hands
Do not allow patient to flex/extend neck during assessment

pediatric_under_8.directive: Use length-based dosing tape (Broselow) if available. Pediatric vital sign ranges differ — tachypnea + tachycardia threshold lower. IO access if IV fails after 2 attempts or 90 sec. Smaller airway size, smaller bag for BVM. Family-centered approach — let parent stay if possible.
do_not: Do not use adult drug doses
Do not separate from parent unless absolutely required
Do not delay transport for prolonged IV attempts — go IO

RED_general.directive: Immediate intervention required. Reassess airway, breathing, circulation continuously. Two large-bore IV access. Cardiac monitor. Prepare for rapid transport to highest-capability available facility. Do not delay scene time more than 10 min for stable interventions.
do_not: Do not engage in extensive on-scene assessment — load and go
Do not skip reassessment cycle (vitals every 5 min minimum)

YELLOW_general.directive: Urgent but stable. Complete primary survey on scene. Establish IV access if indicated. Vitals every 15 min. Transport to appropriate-level facility — does not require highest-trauma center necessarily. Pain control per protocol.
do_not: Do not skip detailed secondary survey
Do not transport before completing splinting / wound dressing if patient is stable

GREEN_general.directive: Walking wounded. Move to designated GREEN holding area. Document patient identity + chief complaint. Recheck for missed injuries at scheduled intervals. Group transport (bus, multi-patient vehicle) acceptable.
do_not: Do not occupy ambulance resources for GREEN unless no other transport
Do not lose track of GREEN patients in chaotic scenes — registration is critical

BLACK_general.directive: Expectant or deceased. Document time, location (GPS if available), brief description. Cover respectfully. Do not occupy transport resources. Inform family liaison / chaplain if available. Coroner / medical examiner per local protocol.
do_not: Do not move body unless required for scene safety
Do not commit ambulance to BLACK transport in active MCI
